fix: Linuxbrew Python이 pip3 shadowing하는 문제 수정 — 절대경로 사용

PATH 앞에 위치한 Linuxbrew bin의 pip3가 PEP 668로 차단됨.
/usr/local/bin/pip3 절대경로로 베이스 이미지의 pip 직접 사용.

fix: Issue #190 NetworkFilterテーブルのDBマイグレーション自動化 (#198)

* fix: Issue #190 DBマイグレーションv7追加でNetworkFilterテーブルを自動作成

## 実装内容
- CURRENT_DB_VERSION を 6 → 7 に更新
- バージョン履歴コメントにv7追加: NetworkFilterConfig, NetworkFilterRuleテーブル作成
- migrateV6ToV7() 関数を追加（NetworkFilterConfig/NetworkFilterRule テーブル作成）
- migrateDatabase() にv6→v7マイグレーションブロックを追加
- createInitialTables() にNetworkFilterConfig/NetworkFilterRuleテーブルを追加（新規DB用）

## 受入基準の達成状況
- [x] v6のDBに対してmigrateDatabase()を実行するとNetworkFilterConfig/NetworkFilterRuleテーブルが作成される
- [x] 新規DBにinitializeDatabase()を実行するとNetworkFilterConfig/NetworkFilterRuleテーブルが作成される
- [x] マイグレーション後のDBバージョンが7になる
- [x] 既存データが失われない（テスト通過で確認）
- [x] 既にv7のDBに対して再実行してもエラーにならない

## 関連ドキュメント
- docs/sdd/tasks/network-filtering/issue-190-db-migration.md
- docs/sdd/design/network-filtering/issue-190-db-migration.md
- docs/sdd/requirements/network-filtering/issue-190-db-migration.md

## テスト
- src/bin/__tests__/cli-utils.test.ts: 全テスト通過

Closes #190

fix: dashboard should use same-origin API calls, not localhost

The dashboard was hardcoded to call http://localhost:3000 when
VITE_API_URL wasn't set, causing login failures when deployed.

Since the dashboard is served by the gateway at /dashboard, it should
use relative URLs to call the gateway's API endpoints at the same origin.

Changed default from 'http://localhost:3000' to '' (same-origin).

This fixes the admin login issue at /dashboard route.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Deploy 5 Swipe Dojo evolution variants (evo-swipe-dojo-2026-03-05-001)

5 parallel teams evolved swipe-dojo with distinct directions:
- T1: Mechanic Depth (distinct enemy behaviors, rage mode)
- T2: Belt Progression (rank system, environment themes)
- T3: Combo Spectacle (last-stand mode, finishing blows, announcer)
- T4: Risk/Reward (counter-attack, power-ups, stage choice)
- T5: Audio/Visual (swipe trails, multi-arrow, reactive music)

All 5 passed Playwright testing with score 80 (zero errors).

commit.message:
Filter out double-barrier "what hits first" crypto markets

These markets (e.g. "Will Bitcoin hit $60k or $80k first?") have two
competing price barriers and non-standard token_ids ("$60k"/"$80k"
instead of "Yes"/"No"). The single-barrier touch probability model
cannot price them correctly (was showing Fair: 100%).

Three-layer filtering:
- update_bot/scanner.py: detect and skip during market discovery
- polymarket.py: skip markets with missing Yes/No token_ids
- full_scan.py: skip questions with "first" + two $ amounts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(income): corrige classificação de créditos com nome do cliente na descrição

Causa raiz: Caixa Econômica e outros bancos incluem o nome/CPF do titular na
descrição de créditos de salário (ex: "CREDITO SALARIO 770.292.327-04 JOAO
SILVA"). O match de nome forte estava excluindo esses créditos como
"autotransferência" — resultado: 422 com "Nenhum crédito válido".

Correções:
- ehRendaLaboral: créditos com keyword de renda laboral (SALARIO, VENCIMENTO,
  FGTS, HONORARIO, etc.) ficam isentos da verificação de autotransferência por
  nome/CPF — o empregador incluir o nome do empregado é comportamento esperado
- Adicionados keywords abreviados Caixa/BB: CRED PIX, CRED TED, CRED DOC,
  CRED SAL, CRED FGTS, CRED INSS, CR PIX, CR TED, CR DOC, DEP IDENT, TR RECEB
- Sufixo D/C (Caixa, BB): "1.250,00C" → crédito; "250,00D" → débito negado
  em add(), VALOR_RE e Strategy 2
- 422 de classificação agora inclui debug_amostra_transacoes (20 primeiras
  transações com descrição, valor e motivo de exclusão) — visível no console

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Harden security: URL validation, honeypot bot trap, tighter CSP, CSRF expansion

- Validate sourceUrl in corridor create/update (reject non-http(s), cap 2048 chars)
- Add input length limits on corridor fields and contact form fields
- Add honeypot field to contact form (silent bot detection)
- Remove unsafe-eval from CSP script-src
- Expand CSRF origin validation to checkout, billing, and events endpoints

commit.message:
Add custom EditSlider, fix Whites/Shadows, undo/redo, and key handling

Replace system Slider with custom playhead-style EditSlider across all
edit controls. Separate Whites from Highlights processing so they affect
distinct tonal ranges. Increase Shadows effect strength (5x). Add
undo/redo via UndoManager with Cmd+Z/Shift+Cmd+Z. Fix M key beep by
handling repeat phase. Fix Esc by using global NSEvent monitor. Add
gradient backgrounds for WB Temp, Tint, and Saturation sliders. Support
Option+drag for 10x precision scrubbing. Extend max Kelvin to 50000.

commit.message:
feat: hybrid OOF evaluation + metabolic label relabeling

OOF evaluation improvements (eval_oof_cv.py):
- Joint (AT x C) grid search fixes sequential-search bug
- Expanded AT grid [0.25-0.50], C grid [0.01, 0.1, 1.0, 10.0]
- LR retrained on train+val (441 samples) after hyperparameter selection
- Added 2 global threshold-free features (overall_mean_prob, overall_std_prob)
- OOF AUC improved: 0.6911 -> 0.7009 (target >=0.70 met)

Metabolic label mode (--metabolic flag):
- New criterion: pH < 7.15 AND BDecf >= 8 (removes respiratory acidemia)
- 48 recordings relabeled negative (respiratory acidemia, clinically benign)
- OOF AUC with metabolic labels: 0.7386, FN reduced 33->18
- Outputs to results/oof_cv_evaluation_metabolic/

Clinical rules engine:
- Added src/rules/ (baseline, variability, decelerations, sinusoidal, tachysystole)
- Added src/features/clinical_extractor.py
- Updated local_eval_cpu.py with correct checkpoint loading (head weights fix)

Documentation:
- docs/azure_training_runs.md: sections 10b, 10c, 10d, updated section 11
- docs/clinical_rules_engine_v8.md, hybrid_clinical_features_v8.md added
- Full FN/FP error analysis with clinical metadata

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: getAnvilScript Windows platform check + rebuild dist (v2.0.32)

getAnvilScript() in framework.ts always looked for anvil.sh regardless
of platform. Now respects process.platform — looks for .ps1/.cmd on
Windows, .sh on Unix. Same fix applied in v2.0.31 for findAnvilScript
in gates.ts.

Also rebuilds dist/ so all v2.0.32 changes (structured logging, Windows
fixes) are compiled and active at runtime.

commit.message:
v0.9.0: Add agent-facing coordination CLI commands

New CLI subcommands for orchestrator agents:
- `task notes <id>` — list notes for a task
- `task help --type question` — agent-answerable interventions
- `intervention list` — list interventions (filter by task_id, status)
- `intervention respond <id>` — respond to interventions programmatically

Updated docs (ORCHESTRATIA.md, SKILL.md) with new endpoints:
- GET /server/tasks/{id}/notes
- GET /server/interventions
- POST /server/interventions/{id}/respond
- WebSocket task subscriptions (subscribe_task/unsubscribe_task)

Version bump: 0.8.14 → 0.9.0 (synced pyproject.toml and __init__.py)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: SOQL Explorer lookup/relationship fields now display values

Relationship fields (e.g. Account__r.Name, Owner.Email) were showing
empty values and incorrect column labels. Root cause: buildColumns()
used getPopulatedFieldsAsMap() which returns relationship objects as
single keys, not nested field paths.

Apex: buildColumns() now recursively traverses nested SObjects for
dot-notation columns. Cursor path uses new buildColumnsFromMap() that
parses raw JSON maps directly (more reliable than SObject deserialization).

JS: Records flattened once on arrival (not per-render) into dot-notation
keys matching the column definitions.

commit.message:
feat: add meaningful comments to JIRA worklog entries referencing GH issue number (#26)

Worklog entries now include a descriptive comment:
- "Copied time spent from GH #N" — first sync (JIRA had 0 time logged) or
  in-place update of the managed worklog
- "Increased time spent from GH #N" — delta worklog when JIRA already has time

The GH issue number is fetched via content.number in the GraphQL query for
both the initial and paginated page requests. Items without an issue number
(draft items) use "GH project item" as the reference instead.

The managed worklog lookup now also recognises the legacy comment
"[managed by track-reporting-date workflow]" from earlier workflow versions.

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix: resolve batch map before statement_timeout in search

The getBatchForTable EXISTS scan on catalog.releases (18.9M rows)
takes >3s on cold cache, which exceeded the statement_timeout and
prevented the in-memory cache from ever being populated. Move batch
resolution before the pinned connection so it's not subject to the
3s timeout.

commit.message:
Fix avatar clipping at 1440px and project-bullet flex collapse

Issue A: at exactly 1440×900 (MacBook "looks like 1440×900" mode) the
max-width:1439px mid-desktop rule was off by 1px, leaving align-items:center
active. When hero content slightly exceeded available height the grid centered
it upward, pushing the avatar behind the nav. Fix: add align-items:start to
the max-width:1440px rule so the content is always anchored to padding-top.

Issue B: .project-bullets li uses display:flex which turns inline <strong>
tags into separate anonymous flex items (3-column appearance). Below 1440px
switch to display:block so <strong> flows naturally inline. .timeline-bullets
is unaffected.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Increase email fetch limits for better coverage after gaps

Inbox live fetch: 25 → 50 threads per page so more emails show at once.
Gmail sync: 100 → 200 threads so All Mail, Smart Inboxes, and AI
analysis cover more history (e.g. after a weekend away). Load More
button still available for pagination beyond the first page.
